---
title: "Euclid × CMB Lensing"
occasion: SPT Secondaries and Cross-Correlations Call
description: Status update on Euclid × ACT/SPT CMB lensing cross-correlations
draft: false
author:
  name: Cail Daley & Euclid CMBX team
date: March 30, 2026
date-format: long

output-file: index.html

format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    fig-align: center
    include-after-body: assets/no_footer_on_titleslide.html
    footer: Cail Daley | Euclid × CMB-κ | SPT Secondaries & Cross-Correlations | March 30, 2026
    history: false
    hash: true
    pdf-separate-fragments: true
    pdf-max-pages-per-slide: 1
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    center-title-slide: false

html-math-method:
  method: mathjax
  url: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mathjax@3/es5/tex-svg-full.js
include-in-header: assets/mathjax.html
---

## Euclid DR1 targets October 2026, <br> but this will be extremely challenging

- ESA hack, B-modes under investigation, unprecedented turnaround

- The **CMBX group in Paris** meets for full-day hacks once a week:
  - Cail Daley, Léa Dumilly, Margherita Lembo, Emma Ayçoberry, Vilasini Tinnaneri Sreekanth, Laura Salvati, Giulio Fabbian

- Building a pipeline for Euclid × CMB lensing and tSZ

- We haven't used SPT $y$-maps yet — Abhi's maps for DR1?

- Giulio talked to someone at NERSC while in Santa Barbara: should be no problem to reserve 10s of GBs for at least the Euclid catalogs

- Making progress but still waiting on a form to give SPT collaborators data access. Sorry!


## Euclid RR2 is unblinded — we're building a full pipeline while we can still poke around

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:55%"}

- ~30% of DR1 area (385 deg²), **unblinded**

- Cross-correlating with $\kappa$ from **ACT DR6** and **SPT-3G winter field**

- Two tracers: $\delta_g \kappa$ and $\gamma \kappa$, **6 tomographic bins**

- Pseudo-$C_\ell$ estimation with NaMaster, Gaussian covariance

:::
::: {.column style="width:45%"}

::: {style="margin-top:-40px"}
![](images/shared_map_conventions_south.png){width=100%}
:::

:::
::::::::::::::

::: {style="margin-top:-70px;position:relative"}
![](images/euclid_cmbx_rr2dr1_footprints.png){fig-align="center" width=90%}
::: {style="font-size:0.72em;text-align:right;margin-top:-35px;margin-right:60px"}
Credit: M. Lembo
:::
:::


## Six tomographic bins

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:50%"}

![](images/euclid_nz_wl.png){width=100%}

- **WL**: ~50M galaxies, two shear pipelines (lensmc, metacal)

:::
::: {.column style="width:50%"}

![](images/euclid_nz_gc.png){width=100%}

- **GC**: ~30M galaxies, 6 bins to $z \sim 0.9$

:::
::::::::::::::


## Galaxy density × CMB lensing ($\delta_g \kappa$)

![](images/signal_overview_density.png){fig-align="center" width=90%}


## Cosmic shear × CMB lensing ($\gamma \kappa$)

![](images/signal_overview_metacal.png){fig-align="center" width=90%}


## tSZ × galaxy clustering with Planck (Léa)

![](images/tSZxGC.png){fig-align="center" width=85%}


## Map vs catalog estimator agree at high $\ell$, diverge at low $\ell$

![](images/euclid_cmbx_catalog_vs_map_lensmc.png){fig-align="center" width=85%}


## Eleven VMPZ-ID systematic maps track observational effects {.scrollable}

![](images/systematic_maps_composite.png){width=100% style="height:9374px"}


## Some signs of systematic contamination {.scrollable}

::: {style="text-align:center"}
$X_\ell^f = \ell \, C_\ell^{\kappa S} C_\ell^{fS} / C_\ell^{SS}$ where $f$ = galaxy field, $S$ = systematic map
:::

![](images/euclid_cmbx_contamination_overview.png){width=100% style="height:4252px"}


## $\ell$-dependence

![](images/fig_ell_range_robustness.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}
